title: Agency In Transition
definition: The ability to act and influence one's life despite feeling trapped in cycles, especially in relation to societal structures.

difficulty_levels.beginner: It's about finding the power within yourself to change your situation, even when it seems impossible or society doesn't understand you.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A state of self-awareness and personal growth where one can assert control over their life trajectory despite societal pressures or limitations.
difficulty_levels.expert: The capacity to disrupt established patterns and create new ones, even when faced with resistance from societal norms or personal attachments. It embodies the Buddhist concept of self-awakening amidst social transformation.

extra_fields.insight: In the face of societal expectations, one can still find a sense of agency that fosters personal growth and contributes to societal change.
